class.name: FmodChannelControl
class.brief_description: Abstract base class that provides common functionality for Channel and ChannelGroup.
class.description: FmodChannelControl is an abstract base class that provides shared functionality for both [FmodChannel] (single channel playback control) and [FmodChannelGroup] (channel mixing group). It handles playback control, volume management, panning, filtering, DSP chain configuration, and sample-accurate scheduling. This class cannot be instantiated directly - use [FmodChannel] or [FmodChannelGroup] instead.

method.name: add_dsp
method.description: Adds a DSP unit to the DSP chain at the specified index. The DSP will be inserted at the given position in the processing chain, affecting how audio is processed.

method.name: add_fade_point
method.description: Adds a sample-accurate fade point at the specified DSP clock time. This allows precise volume automation synchronized to the audio clock. The volume will transition to the specified value at the exact clock time specified.

method.name: channel_control_is_null
method.description: Returns [code]true[/code] if the underlying FMOD::ChannelControl object is null (not initialized or already released).

method.name: channel_control_is_valid
method.description: Returns [code]true[/code] if the underlying FMOD::ChannelControl object is valid and can be used for operations.

method.name: clear_callback
method.description: Clears the callback set by [method set_callback]. After calling this, the [signal callback_received] signal will no longer be emitted when FMOD events occur.

method.name: get_audibility
method.description: Calculates and returns the final audibility of the channel based on all volume attenuation factors. This considers volume levels from the channel itself, its parent channel groups, and any applied 3D attenuation. Returns a value between 0.0 (silent) and 1.0 (full volume).

method.name: get_delay
method.description: Returns a [Dictionary] containing the current delay settings with the following keys: - [code]"start"[/code] ([code]int[/code]): The DSP clock value when playback should start. - [code]"end"[/code] ([code]int[/code]): The DSP clock value when playback should stop. - [code]"stop_channels"[/code] ([code]bool[/code]): Whether to stop channels when the end time is reached.

method.name: get_dsp
method.description: Retrieves the DSP unit at the specified index in the DSP chain. Returns [code]null[/code] if the index is invalid.

method.name: get_dsp_clock
method.description: Returns a [Dictionary] with the current DSP clock values containing: - [code]"dsp_lock"[/code] ([code]int[/code]): The current DSP clock for this channel. - [code]"parent_clock"[/code] ([code]int[/code]): The current DSP clock of the parent ChannelGroup. These clock values are used for sample-accurate scheduling operations.

method.name: get_dsp_index
method.description: Returns the index of the specified DSP unit in the DSP chain, or -1 if the DSP is not found in the chain.

method.name: get_fade_points
method.description: Returns a [Dictionary] containing fade point information with the following keys: - [code]"num_points"[/code] ([code]int[/code]): The number of fade points currently set. - [code]"point_dspclock"[/code] ([code]int[/code]): The DSP clock of the fade point (for single-point queries). - [code]"point_volume"[/code] ([code]float[/code]): The volume level of the fade point (for single-point queries).

method.name: get_low_pass_gain
method.description: Returns the current low pass gain value. This controls how much the dry signal is affected when the built-in lowpass filter is applied.

method.name: get_mode
method.description: Returns the current playback mode flags that control the behavior of this channel. See [enum FmodSystem.FmodMode] for available mode flags.

method.name: get_mute
method.description: Returns [code]true[/code] if the channel is currently muted.

method.name: get_num_dsps
method.description: Returns the number of DSP units currently in the DSP chain for this channel.

method.name: get_paused
method.description: Returns [code]true[/code] if the channel is currently paused.

method.name: get_pitch
method.description: Returns the current relative pitch/playback rate. A value of 1.0 means normal playback speed, values below 1.0 slow down playback, and values above 1.0 speed it up.

method.name: get_reverb_properties
method.description: Returns the wet/send level for the specified reverb instance (0-3). This controls how much of the channel's audio is sent to the reverb effect.

method.name: get_volume_db
method.description: Returns the current volume level in decibels (dB). 0 dB represents unity gain (no change), negative values attenuate the signal, and positive values amplify it.

method.name: get_volume_ramp
method.description: Returns [code]true[/code] if volume changes are ramped smoothly (interpolated over time), or [code]false[/code] if volume changes are applied instantaneously.

method.name: is_playing
method.description: Returns [code]true[/code] if the channel is currently playing audio. Returns [code]false[/code] if the channel has stopped or if the underlying FMOD handle has become invalid.

method.name: remove_dsp
method.description: Removes the specified DSP unit from the DSP chain. The DSP will no longer process audio for this channel.

method.name: remove_fade_points
method.description: Removes all fade points between the specified DSP clock start and end values. This cancels any volume automation scheduled within that time range.

method.name: set_callback
method.description: Sets up a callback for receiving notifications from FMOD about channel events. When enabled, the [signal callback_received] signal will be emitted when FMOD events occur on this channel.

method.name: set_delay
method.description: Sets sample-accurate start and stop times relative to the parent ChannelGroup's DSP clock. This allows precise scheduling of when playback should begin and end. - [param start]: The DSP clock value when playback should start. Use 0 for immediate playback. - [param end]: The DSP clock value when playback should stop. Use 0 to play until manually stopped. - [param stop_channels]: If [code]true[/code], channels will be stopped when the end time is reached.

method.name: set_dsp_index
method.description: Moves the specified DSP unit to a new position (index) in the DSP chain. This changes the order in which DSPs process the audio signal.

method.name: set_fade_point_ramp
method.description: Adds a fade point at the specified DSP clock time with volume ramping. Similar to [method add_fade_point], but the volume transition will use a ramp curve for smoother changes.

method.name: set_low_pass_gain
method.description: Sets the gain of the dry signal when the built-in lowpass filter is applied. This affects how much the original unfiltered signal passes through when filtering is active. Values range from 0.0 to 1.0.

method.name: set_mode
method.description: Sets the playback mode flags that control the behavior of this channel. Mode flags include loop settings (LOOP_OFF, LOOP_NORMAL, LOOP_BIDI), spatial audio settings (2D, 3D with various rolloff types), and creation options (CREATESTREAM, CREATESAMPLE). See [enum FmodSystem.FmodMode] for all available options.

method.name: set_mute
method.description: Sets the mute state of the channel. When muted, the channel produces no audio output but continues processing.

method.name: set_pan
method.description: Adjusts the left/right panning of the channel. - [code]-1.0[/code]: Full left - [code]0.0[/code]: Center (default) - [code]1.0[/code]: Full right Values between these points create intermediate panning positions.

method.name: set_paused
method.description: Sets the pause state of the channel. When paused, playback is temporarily suspended and can be resumed by setting paused to [code]false[/code].

method.name: set_pitch
method.description: Sets the relative pitch/playback rate. - [code]1.0[/code]: Normal playback speed - Values below 1.0: Slower playback (lower pitch) - Values above 1.0: Faster playback (higher pitch)

method.name: set_reverb_properties
method.description: Sets the wet/send level for a specific reverb instance (0-3). This controls how much of the channel's audio is sent to the global reverb effect. Higher values result in more reverberation.

method.name: set_volume_db
method.description: Sets the volume level in decibels (dB). - [code]0.0 dB[/code]: Unity gain (no change) - Negative values: Attenuate (quieter) - Positive values: Amplify (louder) Typical range is -80 dB to +24 dB.

method.name: set_volume_ramp
method.description: Sets whether volume changes should be ramped (interpolated smoothly over time) or applied instantaneously. Ramping prevents clicks and pops when changing volume.

method.name: stop
method.description: Stops playback of the channel (or all channels in nested ChannelGroups). The channel becomes invalid after stopping and cannot be reused.

method.name: set_3d_attributes
method.description: Sets the 3D position and velocity for spatial audio. - [param pos]: Position in world space - [param vel]: Velocity for doppler effect calculations

method.name: get_3d_attributes
method.description: Returns the 3D attributes as a Dictionary with: - [code]pos[/code]: Current position (Vector3) - [code]vel[/code]: Current velocity (Vector3)

method.name: set_3d_cone_orientation
method.description: Sets the orientation of the directional cone for directional audio. The vector should be normalized and point in the direction the sound is facing.

method.name: get_3d_cone_orientation
method.description: Returns the current cone orientation vector.

method.name: set_3d_cone_settings
method.description: Sets the cone parameters for directional audio. - [param inside_cone_angle]: Angle in degrees where volume is at maximum (0-360) - [param outside_cone_angle]: Angle in degrees where volume begins to attenuate (0-360) - [param outside_volume_db]: Volume level outside the cone in dB

method.name: get_3d_cone_settings
method.description: Returns the cone settings as a Dictionary with: - [code]inside_cone_angle[/code]: Inside cone angle in degrees - [code]outside_cone_angle[/code]: Outside cone angle in degrees - [code]outside_volume_db[/code]: Outside volume in dB

method.name: set_3d_custom_rolloff
method.description: Sets a custom distance rolloff curve. Each point is a Vector3 where x=distance, y=volume, z=unused.

method.name: get_3d_custom_rolloff
method.description: Returns the custom rolloff curve points.

method.name: set_3d_distance_filter
method.description: Sets the distance filter parameters. - [param custom]: Enable custom filter settings - [param custom_level]: Filter level when custom is enabled - [param center_freq]: Center frequency of the distance filter

method.name: get_3d_distance_filter
method.description: Returns the distance filter settings as a Dictionary with: - [code]custom[/code]: Whether custom filter is enabled - [code]custom_level[/code]: Custom filter level - [code]center_freq[/code]: Center frequency

method.name: set_3d_doppler_level
method.description: Sets the doppler effect intensity for this channel. Higher values increase the pitch shift due to velocity.

method.name: get_3d_doppler_level
method.description: Returns the current doppler level.

method.name: set_3d_level
method.description: Sets the 3D panning level (0.0 to 1.0). 0.0 = full 2D panning, 1.0 = full 3D spatialization.

method.name: get_3d_level
method.description: Returns the current 3D panning level.

method.name: set_3d_min_distance
method.description: Sets the minimum distance for 3D attenuation. Inside this distance, the sound plays at full volume.

method.name: get_3d_min_distance
method.description: Returns the minimum 3D distance.

method.name: set_3d_max_distance
method.description: Sets the maximum distance for 3D attenuation. Beyond this distance, the sound volume stays at the minimum level.

method.name: get_3d_max_distance
method.description: Returns the maximum 3D distance.

method.name: set_3d_occlusion
method.description: Sets the occlusion factors for direct and reverb paths. - [param direct_occlusion]: 0.0 (no occlusion) to 1.0 (fully occluded) - [param reverb_occlusion]: 0.0 (no occlusion) to 1.0 (fully occluded)

method.name: get_3d_occlusion
method.description: Returns the occlusion values as a Dictionary with: - [code]direct_occlusion[/code]: Direct path occlusion (0.0-1.0) - [code]reverb_occlusion[/code]: Reverb path occlusion (0.0-1.0)

method.name: set_3d_spread
method.description: Sets the stereo spread angle for 3D positioned sounds. Higher values make the sound more diffuse in the stereo field.

method.name: get_3d_spread
method.description: Returns the current 3D spread angle.

method.name: set_mix_levels_input
method.description: Sets the input mix levels for each channel of a multi-channel sound. This allows per-channel volume control.

method.name: set_mix_levels_output
method.description: Sets the output mix levels for each speaker channel. Allows custom routing to specific speaker outputs.

method.name: set_mix_matrix
method.description: Sets a custom mix matrix that maps input channels (columns) to output speaker channels (rows). The matrix is row-major. If [param inchannel_hop] is [code]0[/code], each row is assumed to contain [param inchannels] values.

method.name: get_mix_matrix
method.description: Returns the current final mix matrix as a Dictionary with [code]matrix[/code], [code]outchannels[/code], [code]inchannels[/code], and [code]inchannel_hop[/code].

members.member: The gain of the dry signal when the built-in lowpass filter is applied. Controls how much unfiltered signal passes through when filtering is active (0.0 to 1.0).
If [code]true[/code], the channel is muted and produces no audio output.
If [code]true[/code], the channel playback is paused.
The relative pitch/playback rate. 1.0 is normal speed, values below 1.0 slow down playback, values above 1.0 speed it up.
The volume level in decibels (dB). 0 dB is unity gain, negative values attenuate, positive values amplify.
If [code]true[/code], volume changes are ramped smoothly over time. If [code]false[/code], volume changes are instantaneous.

signal.name: callback_received
signal.description: Emitted when a callback is received from FMOD. The [param type] parameter indicates the type of callback event that occurred. Connect to this signal to receive notifications about channel events such as sound finished playing, etc.
